name: Heights Finance
description_short: Heights Finance offers installment personal loans up to $10,000 across 15+ states with in-branch service and flexible underwriting beyond credit scores.
description_long:
Heights Finance is a consumer lending company operating physical branch locations across multiple states including Tennessee, Alabama, Georgia, Kentucky, Mississippi, Missouri, Louisiana, Oklahoma, and others. The company specializes in personal installment loans with maximum loan amounts up to $10,000, positioning itself as a lender that works with borrowers who may not qualify for traditional bank financing.

The company's core service is installment personal loans available through both in-branch visits and online application. They advertise fast funding (check in hand within 24 hours based on customer reviews) and flexible underwriting that considers factors beyond credit scores. Heights Finance also offers ancillary services including credit insurance products, home and auto insurance referrals, and a "Silver Safeguard" product. The company has modernized its payment infrastructure with a mobile app and eliminated cash-only payments at branches, offering multiple payment methods instead.

Heights Finance distinguishes itself through emphasis on customer service and respectful treatment, marketing the tagline that borrowers are "much more than just a credit score." The company operates a physical branch network which allows for in-person loan origination and customer service. They also offer a referral program ($30 per successful referral), a financial literacy program called "MoneySKILL," and debt consolidation/refinancing options. Customer reviews consistently highlight individual loan officers by name and praise service quality.

The website provides minimal information about APR ranges, specific loan terms, or underwriting criteria, which is typical for consumer installment lenders in this market segment. The high TrustScore (4.7/5 from 21,518 reviews) and overwhelmingly positive customer testimonials suggest operational reliability, though this reflects self-selected review data. As with most installment lenders, actual loan costs and terms likely vary significantly by applicant credit profile and state lending regulations.
address: 2209 W I-240 Service Road Ste 311, Oklahoma City, OK 73159

company_info.headquarters: 2209 W I-240 Service Road Ste 311, Oklahoma City, OK 73159

pros: Fast funding with documented same-day or 24-hour check delivery based on customer reviews
Physical branch network in 15+ states allowing in-person service and relationship building
Flexible underwriting explicitly stated to consider factors beyond credit scores
High customer satisfaction rating (4.7/5 from 21,518 verified reviews) with consistent staff praise
Multiple payment options including mobile app, eliminating cash-only transactions
Additional products including credit insurance and debt consolidation/refinancing options
Referral incentive program ($30 per successful friend referral)
cons: Website provides no disclosed APR ranges, making true cost of borrowing impossible to assess before application
Maximum loan amount of $10,000 is modest compared to some competitors offering $25,000-$50,000
Limited geographic footprint (15 states) with branch availability dependent on location
No transparent eligibility criteria published, requiring applicants to contact company for pre-qualification
"Cash-free branch" notation suggests recent operational changes that may affect payment flexibility
best_for: Borrowers with imperfect credit seeking fast personal loans who value in-person service
Customers needing loans under $10,000 for consolidation, auto repair, or unexpected expenses

Applicants in the company's service states (Tennessee, Alabama, Georgia, Kentucky, Mississippi, Missouri, Louisiana, Oklahoma, Indiana, Illinois, South Carolina, Texas, Wisconsin) preferring branch-based lending
Consumers willing to trade potentially higher rates for faster approval and flexible underwriting
diagnosis: Heights Finance is best suited for borrowers in its service states seeking personal loans under $10,000 who value fast funding and in-person service over online-only lenders. The critical caveat is that no APR or actual cost information is disclosed on the website—applicants must contact the company directly to understand true loan costs, making rate comparison difficult before commitment.
services: Mobile app for loan management and payments
In-branch loan origination and customer service
